title: GitHub Pages site with Free SSL/TLS
description: A complete guide to configuring a custom domain with free SSL/TLS for GitHub Pages using Cloudflare.
content:
## Background

GitHub Pages sites support the ability to use custom domains.

Custom domains allow you to serve your site from a domain other than USERNAME.github.io.

However, by default, GitHub Pages sites use the HTTP protocol, meaning that if you use a custom domain, your website will not be accessible via HTTPS.

HTTPS certificates are paid, so you will need to purchase one if you require HTTPS.

Cloudflare offers free SSL/TLS certificate solutions, which can be used for free.

However, the current domain was purchased from GoDaddy, so it's necessary to bind all three together to solve the Custom Domain + HTTPS issue.

## Switching GoDaddy Namespaces to Cloudflare's Nameservers

Cloudflare provides free SSL/TLS certificates, and switching to Cloudflare's Nameservers will fulfill this requirement.

When you switch your domain's Nameservers from GoDaddy to Cloudflare's Nameservers, the following changes will occur in the network topology:

* Using GoDaddy Nameservers with GoDaddy:

1. DNS Resolution Authority: When other servers or clients try to find the IP address corresponding to your domain name, they will query GoDaddy's Nameservers to obtain this information. GoDaddy's Nameservers are the authoritative source of your domain's DNS records.

2. DNS Management: You manage all DNS settings in your GoDaddy control panel, such as adding or modifying A records, CNAME records, MX records, etc.

3. Traffic Routing: When users visit your website, their requests are directly routed to the server IP address you specify in your DNS records.

* After switching to Cloudflare's Nameservers:

1. DNS Resolution Authority: Cloudflare's Nameservers become the authoritative source of DNS records for your domain. This means that when other servers or clients look up your domain, they will now query Cloudflare.

2. DNS Management: You now manage your DNS records in Cloudflare's control panel. This includes modifying or adding new DNS records, setting page rules, configuring SSL/TLS, managing caching, and more.

3. Traffic Routing and Security: When users visit your website, their requests pass through Cloudflare's global network. Cloudflare acts as a proxy between your website and visitors, providing additional security (such as DDoS protection and Web Application Firewall) and performance optimizations (such as Content Delivery Network (CDN) and intelligent routing). 4. Caching and Content Distribution: Cloudflare's CDN caches your website's static resources, which are then delivered by edge nodes closer to users. This reduces latency and speeds up content loading.

5. Additional Features: Cloudflare offers many additional features, such as automated SSL certificate processing, network analytics, custom page rules, and traffic control, all of which can be configured in the Cloudflare control panel.

### Locating Cloudflare Nameservers

1. Create a domain

2. DNS Records

3. Locate Cloudflare Nameservers

4. Copy and save

### Switching Nameservers

Switch GoDaddy's Nameservers to Cloudflare's Nameservers

1. Log in to GoDaddy

2. Go to Domain Management

3. Click "DNS"

4. Click "Nameservers" -> "Change Nameservers"

5. Select "User my own nameservers" and enter the Nameservers obtained from Cloudflare

6. Click "Save"

The domain's Nameservers have now been switched, and GoDaddy can be closed.

## Setting a Custom Domain in GitHub Pages

1. Open your GitHub repository and go to "Settings".

2. Select "Pages" in the sidebar.

3. In the "Custom domain" section, enter your GoDaddy domain name, such as example.com.

4. If needed, check "Enforce HTTPS" to force the use of HTTPS connections.

5. Save these settings.

6. GitHub will provide a CNAME file, or you will need to manually add a CNAME file with your domain name in the repository root directory or the docs/ directory.

## Adding a Custom Domain on Cloudflare

When configuring a custom domain for GitHub Pages on Cloudflare, correctly setting up DNS records is a crucial step in ensuring that HTTPS certificates are generated correctly. According to GitHub's official documentation, here is a guide on how to fill in CNAME and ALIAS or ANAME records:

> GitHub Documentation [Configuring an Apex Domain](https://docs.github.com/en/pages/configuring-a-custom-domain-for-your-github-pages-site/managing-a-custom-domain-for-your-github-pages-site#configuring-an-apex-domain)

* For Apex domains (naked domains, such as example.com):

1. A Record:

Type: A

Name: @ (or your naked domain example.com, @ represents the naked domain)

Value: A list of IP addresses for GitHub Pages

2. AAAA Record:

Type: AAAA

Name: @ (or your naked domain example.com)

Value: A list of IPv6 addresses for GitHub Pages

3. ALIAS or ANAME Record (if your DNS provider supports it):

Type: ALIAS or ANAME

Name: @ (Or your bare domain example.com)

Value: USERNAME.github.io or ORGANIZATION.github.io (replace with your GitHub username or organization name)

* For subdomains (such as www.example.com, blog.example.com):

1. CNAME record:

Type: CNAME

Name: SUBDOMAIN (e.g., www or blog)

Value: USERNAME.github.io or ORGANIZATION.github.io (replace with your GitHub username or organization name)

> This is optional. If you are using the bare domain example.com, you do not need to fill this in. A/AAA uses the top-level domain example.com by default.

The method for setting up DNS records on Cloudflare is as follows:

1. Log in to your Cloudflare account.

2. Select the relevant domain.

3. Click the "DNS" tab.

4. According to the required type (A, AAAA, CNAME, ALIAS, or ANAME), click the "Add Record" button.

5. Fill in the corresponding "Type," "Name," and "Value" fields.

6. Save the record.

Note: Cloudflare does not support traditional ALIAS or ANAME record types. However, Cloudflare provides a similar record called "CNAME Flattening," which works on the root domain just like ALIAS or ANAME records. If you set a CNAME record for the root domain, Cloudflare will automatically apply CNAME Flattening.

## Triggering GitHub HTTPS Certificate Generation

1. If you have just added or modified a custom domain, you may need to remove and re-add your custom domain in the Pages settings of your GitHub repository to trigger the GitHub SSL certificate generation process.

2. Go to "Settings" in your GitHub repository.

3. In the "Pages" section, remove the current custom domain, then re-add it and save.

## Testing

You can use the Observatory feature under Speed ​​provided by Cloudflare to perform regular website testing. Simply set the Frequency to weekly.
